title: Podman 2.0.6 pull and run fails on ubuntu image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When doing a podman pull or a podman run on docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest it fails.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ```podman pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest``` or ```podman run --rm -it docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest bash```

**Describe the results you received:**
For pull
```
bob@server1:~> podman pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d72e567cc804 done  
Copying blob 0f3630e5ff08 done  
Copying blob b6a83d81d1f4 done  
Copying config 9140108b62 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
  Error processing tar file(exit status 1): there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 100:0 for /var/cache/apt/archives/partial): lchown /var/cache/apt/archives/partial: invalid argument
Error: unable to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:d72e567cc804d0b637182ba23f8b9ffe101e753a39bf52cd4db6b89eb089f13b": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 100:0 for /var/cache/apt/archives/partial): lchown /var/cache/apt/archives/partial: invalid argument
bob@server1:~>
```
For run
```
bob@server1:~> podman run --rm -it ubuntu bash
Trying to pull registry.opensuse.org/ubuntu...
  name unknown
Trying to pull docker.io/library/ubuntu...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 0f3630e5ff08 done  
Copying blob d72e567cc804 done  
Copying blob b6a83d81d1f4 done  
Copying config 9140108b62 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
  Error processing tar file(exit status 1): there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 100:0 for /var/cache/apt/archives/partial): lchown /var/cache/apt/archives/partial: invalid argument
Error: unable to pull ubuntu: 2 errors occurred:
	* Error initializing source docker://registry.opensuse.org/ubuntu:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.opensuse.org/ubuntu: name unknown
	* Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:d72e567cc804d0b637182ba23f8b9ffe101e753a39bf52cd4db6b89eb089f13b": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): there might not be enough IDs available in the namespace (requested 100:0 for /var/cache/apt/archives/partial): lchown /var/cache/apt/archives/partial: invalid argument


bob@server1:~>
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
pull and run should of completed with success.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Happens 100% of the time.
 
I'm not an admin on the system this is happening on so I'm working with the system admins to figure things out. One observation I had after reading this https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#solution-8 is my account is not in /etc/passwd. I believe we have ldap setup so I'm not sure if that doesn't leverage /etc/passwd at all, I will need to ask our system admins.  Would this cause issues?  It's odd that my commands complain about not enough IDs in the namespace yet in subgid and subuid it looks fine to me.  Seems like it's not actually using those values. 

Here is output of subgid and subuid
```
bob@server1:~> cat /etc/{subgid,subuid}
adm:100000:65536
bob:214009:65536
adm:100000:65536
bob:214009:65536
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.6
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Tue Sep  8 08:00:00 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
bob@server1:~> rpm -q podman
podman-2.0.6-4.25.1.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical server
```
bob@server1:~> cat /etc/os-release
NAME="SLES"
VERSION="15-SP2"
VERSION_ID="15.2"
PRETTY_NAME="SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP2"
ID="sles"
ID_LIKE="suse"
ANSI_COLOR="0;32"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:suse:sles:15:sp2"
```

comments.body:
Your account most likely does not have entries in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid` to allocate additional UIDs and GIDs for your user to use - see https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/rootless-podman

You can either create the entries and then run `podman system migrate` to cause Podman to re-read the file and use the entries, or set the `ignore_chown_errors` storage option to use only a single UID and GID (and the restrictions that come with that).

I do have those entries

```
bob@server1:~> cat /etc/{subgid,subuid}
adm:100000:65536
bob:214009:65536
adm:100000:65536
bob:214009:65536
```

Podman sees the `/etc/subgid` entry but not the `/etc/subuid` one (the `idMappings` section of `podman info`). The entry in `subuid` seems correct. I would recommend trying a `podman system migrate` and then re-pulling the image to see if that resolves matters; otherwise, the `newuidmap` binary could be missing a file capability, perhaps?

Migrate didn't seem to help. Still same error message.

```
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 21176
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 214009
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 214009
      size: 1
```
I ran podman system migrate as my non-root user account.  Does this need to be executed as root?
No, it's meant to be run as the user running rootless Podman. It causes the rootless user namespace to be re-created, which re-reads the subuid and subgid files.

Actually, I do notice a problem in your `/etc/subuid` - there's an overlap between the range assigned to you and UIDs and GIDs in use in the system (your UID, specifically). The ranges assigned in subuid and subgid should never overlap UIDs and GIDs in use by actual users in the system. I think that you'll need to adjust these to ranges that are not in use, and then run `podman system migrate` to force a re-read of the files.
Thanks for the help! I've got a message out to our system admin to fix that up. I'll reply back with the results once they make the needed change.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I do not believe this is an issue with Podman. Reopen if I am mistaken.
@bobhenkel Can you please post what's the fix for the issue ?

@gokamal The suggestion by mheon solved this.  There were overlapping id values. Sorry I didn't reply back sooner,  after this was resolved. I bounce around a lot and lose track some times.
> Actually, I do notice a problem in your `/etc/subuid` - there's an overlap between the range assigned to you and UIDs and GIDs in use in the system (your UID, specifically). The ranges assigned in subuid and subgid should never overlap UIDs and GIDs in use by actual users in the system. I think that you'll need to adjust these to ranges that are not in use, and then run `podman system migrate` to force a re-read of the files.
Ok thanks, got it fixed along with `podman system migrate`
